question.text: Given USD/JPY = 148.50/148.55 and USD/INR = 83.20/83.25, what is the offer rate for JPY/INR (per 100 JPY)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** To BUY 100 JPY (offer side for JPY/INR): sell INR to buy USD at offer (83.25), then sell USD to get JPY at bid (148.50). Price of 100 JPY = (83.25/148.50) × 100 = 56.0606 ≈ 56.06. **The Trap:** Using wrong bid/offer combination. The JPY/INR bid = USD/INR bid / USD/JPY offer = 83.20/148.55 = 56.01. **Cross-Exam Context:** JPY/INR cross rates are unique because USD is base in both pairs, requiring division instead of multiplication. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When USD is base currency in BOTH underlying pairs, divide USD/INR by USD/JPY to get JPY/INR (per unit or per 100).
